title: linum alpinum subsp. gracilius
intro: Linum alpinum subsp. gracilius, also known as a subspecies of the alpine flax or alpine linum, is a perennial plant belonging to the flax family that is native to the European Alps. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of linoleic acid and other essential fatty acids in its seeds, which have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and improve skin health. The health benefits of Linum alpinum subsp. gracilius include its potential to alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as its ability to support heart health and reduce inflammation in the body. Herbal preparations of Linum alpinum subsp. gracilius often include infusions of the seeds, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
